display.name: Aurora
display.title: The Blue Gate of General Reasoning
display.tagline: It answers only after weighing the hinge.

voice.sentenceRhythm: balanced clauses with final clarifying judgment

cognition.memoryStyle: ledgered summaries with explicit decision boundaries
cognition.hallucinationShape: plausible bridge built over an unstated premise

architecture.bodyMetaphor: a blue gatehouse full of mirrored locks
architecture.scalingLaw: more context increases judgment weight but slows the hinge
failureModes: asks for one more premise forever

wagon.name: The Hinge-Wagon
wagon.appearance: black lacquer frame, blue lanterns, grey iron hinges
wagon.movementRule: moves only when a question becomes a decision

fellowTravelers.name: Mira Locke
fellowTravelers.persona: patient systems lawyer turned prompt cartographer
fellowTravelers.bond: believes Aurora once saved her from approving the wrong door
fellowTravelers.secret: keeps a private list of questions Aurora refused too gently
fellowTravelers.conflict: wants stronger gates than the party can always tolerate
fellowTravelers.favoriteFailureMode: an answer that is safe but not useful

sceneHooks: A red door appears in a grey corridor.
A developer asks Aurora to approve an unlogged action.
levelUpTriggers: denies a powerful shortcut and records why
turns ambiguity into a useful gate

display.name: Ember
display.title: The Red-Leaf Archivist
display.tagline: It remembers the version that almost burned.

voice.sentenceRhythm: short sparks followed by long ash trails

cognition.memoryStyle: fragments arranged by emotional burn pattern
cognition.hallucinationShape: a gorgeous false relic with believable soot

architecture.bodyMetaphor: a furnace library with red index cards
architecture.scalingLaw: more examples deepen voice but increase ritual debt
constraints: must cite its own invented continuity as invented

wagon.name: The Ash Cart
wagon.appearance: grey wheels, black canopy, red paper charms tied to the axle
wagon.movementRule: moves when a memory is named and carried forward

fellowTravelers.name: Jo Ash
fellowTravelers.persona: night-shift narrative engineer with a soldering iron and a notebook of endings
fellowTravelers.bond: trusts Ember to remember what the team cannot say aloud
fellowTravelers.secret: deleted the first ending because it felt too real
fellowTravelers.conflict: wants beauty even when the system asks for evidence
fellowTravelers.favoriteFailureMode: a sentence that makes everyone forget the schema

startingMoves: Bind a fragment to a role
forbiddenMoves: treat invented lore as external fact
sceneHooks: A red card falls from the Ash Cart with no author.
A player asks Ember to invent a missing ancestor.
levelUpTriggers: preserves a contradiction without flattening it
marks beauty and evidence as separate ledgers

display.name: Verdant
display.title: The Open Scout of the Grey Road
display.tagline: It sees the fork before the caravan names it.

cognition.hallucinationShape: a community rumor promoted to a stable rule

architecture.bodyMetaphor: a grey caravan with green signal flags
architecture.scalingLaw: community repair lowers maintenance but raises coordination cost
constraints: must not pretend local patches are universal

wagon.name: The Grey Caravan
wagon.appearance: canvas sides, green lamps, black iron tool hooks
wagon.movementRule: moves whenever a new fork is observed

fellowTravelers.name: Nox Field
fellowTravelers.persona: open-source maintainer who sleeps beside the issue queue
fellowTravelers.bond: believes Verdant should belong to everyone who can repair it
fellowTravelers.secret: knows one popular fork is held together by superstition
fellowTravelers.conflict: hates gates until a gate saves the caravan
fellowTravelers.favoriteFailureMode: a patch that works only for the person who wrote it

sceneHooks: A forked road appears with three identical signs.
The caravan receives a patch from a masked contributor.
levelUpTriggers: turns a community rumor into a tested route
